text: I arrive as startup CTO comparing code hosting and AI coding claims. My past: I need developer speed, but AI and security claims need proof before I stake my team on them.
Today I want to: Understand whether the site gives enough proof to investigate further.
My life goal is: Protect my startup runway by choosing developer infrastructure that compounds.
I have 100 life-years. Each meaningful action costs 1 month(s); each committed dollar costs 1 year(s).

label: arrival
title: GitHub · Change is constant. GitHub keeps you ahead. · GitHub

text: I see "GitHub · Change is constant. GitHub keeps you ahead. · GitHub".
I think: I only have the page title, so I cannot judge the claims yet.
My reaction: My reaction is guarded impatience; GitHub is relevant, but the proof is not visible to me yet.
Idea: put concrete trust signals and outcome proof above the fold for technical buyers.
Life-cost decision: Spending 1 month to continue is acceptable only because this could help protect my startup runway by choosing infrastructure that compounds.
Truth assessment: My honest judgment is that there is not enough visible evidence yet; confidence high because I cannot see the page content.
